summary: Re-linked the gm area and adjusted build/config/header fallout from that relink. The PR regenerated very large asm slices for gm_1A36, gmmain_lib, and lbbgflash, moved game-mode struct definitions out of gmmain_lib.h into gm/types.h with a small forward.h, changed configure object statuses for gm/mp sources, and made small source/tooling changes needed to keep the link and symbol replacement workflow coherent. There were no PR body comments, review comments, or reviews in the slice, so intent beyond the title and diff is inferred from changed files.

key_files.reason: Massive regenerated asm slice for the gm relink; gained `.file "gm_1A36.c"` and many updated labels/objects.
Regenerated asm for gmmain_lib as part of the relink, while obj_files.mk kept the asm object rather than the C object.
Large regenerated asm fallout adjacent to the gm relink/sdata2 boundary shift.
Adjusted gm_1A36 and ground `.sdata2` split boundary from 0x804DAC80 to 0x804DAC90.
Changed object classifications: mpcoll.c Unknown to NonMatching, gmmain_lib.c Unknown to NonMatching, gmmain.c Unknown to Matching, and gm_1A36.c Unknown to NonMatching.
Removed the source object entry for src/melee/gm/gmmain_lib.c.o, leaving asm/melee/gm/gmmain_lib.s.o in the text object list.
Reworked init_spr_unk and main-local unused data so gmmain.c could be marked Matching while preserving a non-Gekko NOT_IMPLEMENTED fallback.
Removed an inline MUST_MATCH asm version of gmMainLib_8015D984, kept the C nonmatching implementation, and made gmMainLib_8015D888 static locally.
Shrank the header by moving struct definitions to gm/types.h and converting prototypes to address-comment style.
New home for GameRules, gmm_x0, gmm_x1868, gmm_x1CB0, gmm_x1F2C, gmm_x2FF8, and gm return-value structs.
New forward declaration header for struct GameRules.
Added --keep/-k option so reverting asm can optionally keep the existing obj_files.mk entry.
Expanded identifier detection to include `.` for symbol replacement.

what_changed: The gm library was re-linked, producing very large regenerated assembly diffs in asm/melee/gm/gm_1A36.s, asm/melee/gm/gmmain_lib.s, and asm/melee/lb/lbbgflash.s.
configure.py now classifies gmmain.c as Matching, and gmmain_lib.c, gm_1A36.c, and mpcoll.c as NonMatching instead of Unknown.
obj_files.mk stopped listing src/melee/gm/gmmain_lib.c.o and continued listing asm/melee/gm/gmmain_lib.s.o, indicating the build should still use the asm object for that piece.
A minimal src/melee/gm/gm_1A36.c was added with placeholder include and a static prototype comment for gm_800A4310/gm_801A4310-era work; evidence shows only a stub, not a decompiled implementation.

gmmain.c changed init_spr_unk from a MUST_MATCH-only inline split into a single static function with MWERKS_GEKKO assembly inside and NOT_IMPLEMENTED outside, and kept the unused format string/local array unconditionally in main.

gmmain_lib.c removed a large inline asm MUST_MATCH implementation of gmMainLib_8015D984, retained the C version marked by a decomp.me scratch comment as having a register swap, and made gmMainLib_8015D888 a static file-local declaration.

gmmain_lib.h was substantially cleaned: struct definitions were removed into gm/types.h, prototypes were reformatted with address comments, and gmMainLib_804D3EE0 remained declared as extern struct gmm_x0*.
gm/types.h was introduced for game-mode data structures, including GameRules, gmm_x0, gmm_x1868, gmm_x1CB0, gmm_x1F2C, gmm_x2FF8, UnkFlagStruct2, and several gmm_retval structs.
gm/forward.h was introduced with a forward declaration for GameRules to reduce header coupling.
splits.txt moved the .sdata2 boundary between gm_1A36.c and ground.c by 0x10 bytes, from 0x804DAC80 to 0x804DAC90.
mpcoll.c include cleanup narrowed dolphin/os.h to dolphin/os/OSError.h and removed baselib/gobj.h.
revert_asm.py gained a --keep/-k flag to avoid rewriting obj_files.mk during asm reversion.
rust-utils identifier parsing now treats `.` as an identifier character, which matters for dotted assembly/local-symbol-style names.
smart_moves: Separated gm type definitions from gmmain_lib.h into gm/types.h, making the public function header smaller and reducing accidental type churn in callers.
Added gm/forward.h for GameRules rather than forcing full gm type definitions everywhere a pointer or declaration is enough.
Used address comments on prototypes in gmmain_lib.h and gm_1A36.h, which improves searchability and keeps symbol-to-address mapping visible during decomp.
Kept non-Gekko portability behavior in gmmain.c with NOT_IMPLEMENTED while allowing the MWERKS_GEKKO path to retain inline SPR setup needed for matching.

Removed the inline asm fallback for gmMainLib_8015D984 from the C source instead of carrying duplicated asm/C bodies under MUST_MATCH, clarifying that the remaining source is the active nonmatching decomp attempt.
Added revert_asm.py --keep so relink/revert workflows can be more surgical when obj_files.mk should not be automatically edited.
Updated symbol replacement identifier rules to include '.', a practical fix for assembly/local-label-like symbol names encountered during relinks.
decomp_lessons: Relinking a large module can require coordinated changes across asm dumps, split boundaries, configure object classifications, object-file lists, source stubs, and headers; do not treat regenerated asm as isolated noise.
When a file is not ready to compile as the linked object, keep the asm object in obj_files.mk even if configure.py knows about the C file as NonMatching.
Moving unknown-but-shared structs into a subsystem types.h is useful once multiple gm files need the same layout knowledge; leave placeholders and offset-named fields rather than inventing semantics.

A matching entry point may depend on preserving apparently unused locals or strings; gmmain.c kept `char* unused_format_string = "Data %lx\n";` and a `u32 _[2]` local after removing the MUST_MATCH guard.
For platform-specific inline assembly, prefer a single function with compiler/platform guards around the implementation rather than duplicating the function definition under broad matching guards.
Header exports should not include static/internal helpers; gmMainLib_8015D888 moved to a static declaration in gmmain_lib.c instead of remaining a public TODO-static prototype.
Split-boundary changes as small as 0x10 in .sdata2 can be a legitimate part of a relink and must be reflected in the GALE01 splits.
naming_conventions: Game-mode library functions continue using the `gmMainLib_80xxxxxx` address-based naming style.
The gm_1A36 symbols continue using `gm_801Axxxx` address-based names, with address comments such as `/* 1A4510 */` in headers.
Unknown gm data structures use offset-derived names such as `gmm_x0`, `gmm_x1868`, `gmm_x1CB0`, `gmm_x1F2C`, and return structs like `gmm_retval_ED98`.
Unknown fields preserve offset-style names such as `x1850`, `x1A18`, `x1CB0`, and padding arrays named by offset ranges.
Forward declarations live in `forward.h`; concrete subsystem layouts live in `types.h`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Regenerated asm files include compiler-oriented annotations such as `.file "gm_1A36.c"` and many `.obj` entries, indicating a relink/regeneration pass rather than hand-edited instruction changes.
gmmain.c was promoted to Matching by preserving codegen-relevant unused stack data and moving inline SPR assembly under `MWERKS_GEKKO` inside a normal static function.

gmMainLib_8015D984 remained as C with a decomp.me scratch note saying `register swap`; the prior inline asm MUST_MATCH body was removed, so future work should focus on resolving the C register allocation mismatch rather than maintaining inline asm.
The build configuration distinguished source classification from actual linked object choice: configure.py marks gmmain_lib.c NonMatching, while obj_files.mk keeps the asm gmmain_lib object.
The rust symbol replacement helper now treats dots as part of identifiers, useful when replacing assembly symbols or labels that include dot components.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile or match gmMainLib_8015D984 after the register-swap note.
Find later PRs that fill in src/melee/gm/gm_1A36.c beyond the three-line placeholder stub.
Search for uses of gm/types.h and gm/forward.h to see whether the type split reduced include churn.
Check later relink PRs for reuse of revert_asm.py --keep and rust-utils dotted identifier handling.
Investigate why lbbgflash.s was regenerated during the gm relink and whether the .sdata2 boundary shift caused it.
